title: Quotinator Source Manifest
description: Ordered list of source files to import from a data/sources/ directory. Files are imported in the order listed; files not listed are appended alphabetically after listed entries. Each file entry is one of three kinds: local (file/name only, no provenance or download link), GitHub (a github object, from which url and downloadUrl are computed), or external URI (a plain url, optionally with a separate downloadUrl).

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference — used by editors for validation and IntelliSense.

duplicateResolution.description: How the seeder handles duplicate records when the same primary key appears in more than one file. Omit to inherit the policy from application configuration (Quotinator:DuplicateResolution). When present, this section overrides all configuration-level settings for this directory.

default.description: Policy applied to all entity types that do not have a type-specific override.

file.description: Filename relative to this manifest's directory (e.g. 'vilaboim_movie-quotes.json').

name.description: Human-readable source identifier shown in the import log and management UI (e.g. 'vilaboim/movie-quotes').

url.description: Upstream URL for this source file, for human-readable provenance (e.g. the GitHub repository homepage). When present, the seeder records the import batch as type 'seed' and stores the URL for provenance. Mutually exclusive with github — use github for GitHub-hosted sources instead.

downloadUrl.description: Direct, fetchable URL to the raw source file — used by the auto-update mechanism (Quotinator__AutoUpdateSources) to refresh this file. Only meaningful alongside url (external source) or github (where it is computed, not set directly).

path.description: File path within the repo, relative to its root.

branch.description: Branch or tag to fetch from. Defaults to main if omitted — set explicitly for repos still on master or any other branch.

github.description: GitHub coordinates this source is fetched from. url and downloadUrl are computed from these using the standard github.com and raw.githubusercontent.com conventions — do not also set url/downloadUrl on the same entry; use a plain url for non-GitHub external sources instead.

policy.description: skip — keep the first occurrence and ignore later files with the same ID. overwrite — replace the existing record with the version from the later file.
